items.why: A classic faux ami. Actuellement looks like 'actually' but means CURRENTLY. To say 'actually' use en fait or en réalité. These false friends regularly cost marks.
Students use 'ends in -e = feminine' as a rule. Many exceptions exist: le problème, le système, le programme, le poème - all masculine despite the -e ending. Gender must often be memorised.

items.q: When does the past participle agree with avoir verbs?
items.correct: When the direct object comes BEFORE the verb (la pomme que j'ai mangée).
items.why: Students learn 'avoir = no agreement' as a blanket rule. The exception is PRECEDING DIRECT OBJECT: 'Je l'ai mangée' (la pomme) requires the participle to agree with the object pronoun.

items.q: In which year did Algeria gain independence from France?
items.why: Correct answer: 1962. “1954” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which is NOT part of the Maghreb?
items.why: It's Sénégal. “Algérie” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Quebec's Law 101 (Charter of the French Language) passed?
items.why: The real figure is 1977. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
